result._a_passed_b_rejected_cell: empty — no survivor's edge was an artifact of breaking optional<->core

negative_control_false_acceptance_rate_p._reading: type-I error of the four-rotation acceptance rule ALONE — the multiplicity the max-null removes. The post-max-null false-acceptance rate is a certification-time nested null.

result.run: golden_20260721T221112Z (16 outer folds, A1 full; A2 and B on the 11 A1 survivors)

max_null.null_hypothesis: Optional features carry no incremental information over the frozen core; the observed maximum gain is what searching a pool of candidates produces by itself.
max_null._why_this_unit: A per-rotation null controls only '45 candidates, one maximum, one rotation'. The procedure being defended is larger — four rotations, recurrence of the pick, a majority of positive confirmations, a median clearing the complexity charge — and a null that never reproduces those clauses cannot control the multiplicity they introduce. One permutation therefore produces one null realisation of the WHOLE outer-train optional matrix, and the four rotations are views of that same realisation.
max_null._overlapping_folds: Purged walk-forward inner folds overlap: a row belongs to several of them at once, so 'permute within a fold' is not well defined and the literal requirement 'no block crosses a fold boundary' is unsatisfiable. The boundary that exists and is protected is outer-train against outer-validation; purge and embargo are applied afterwards, when the folds are cut out of the already-permuted object.
max_null._confirmation_is_permuted_too: H0 says the optional features are uninformative EVERYWHERE, so leaving the confirmation fold unpermuted would test the value of selection (does choosing beat picking at random) rather than the absence of information.
max_null.statistic: acceptance.T per arm — the same function, the same code path, that produced the real verdict
max_null.arms_share_permutations: paired results, separate exceedance counters, separate verdicts, no shared threshold

null_constructions._shared: The three differ ONLY in how the optional matrix is made uninformative; everything downstream is byte-identical, which is what makes them comparable.

a1.name: joint block-alignment null (marginal)
a1.mechanism: global block permutation of the whole optional matrix across outer-train
a1._limitation: also destroys optional <-> core, so this is a MARGINAL alignment null, not the conditional X_optional independent of Y given X_core that the hypothesis names

a2.name: grouped block-alignment null
a2.mechanism: outer-train cut into G chronological macro-segments; blocks permuted only WITHIN a segment
a2.segment_rule: equal BAR span, not equal row count
a2._why_bar_span: An equal-row split would make a quiet stretch span years and a busy one span weeks, which is the opposite of holding the local distribution fixed.
a2._why: A1 can carry a block from a distant volatility regime to the start of the window: local autocorrelation survives but the local distribution does not. A2 keeps the regime and is the more defensive of the two.
a2.role: sensitivity check on the survivors of A1

b.name: conditional residual null
b.mechanism: optional_hat = g(core) cross-fitted; residual = optional - optional_hat; residual grouped-block permuted; optional_null = optional_hat + residual_perm
b.g: multi-output Ridge, core -> all optional columns
b.cross_fitting: 5 contiguous chronological blocks over outer-train with a 35-bar embargo, so a row's fitted value never comes from a model that saw that row and neighbouring rows inside the embargo cannot stand in for it
b._why_not_the_inner_folds: They overlap, so 'trained without this row' would be unenforceable.
b.alpha_selection: held-out reconstruction MSE of the FEATURES; no label, outcome or trading result enters the nuisance model
b._marginal_drift: fitted + permuted residual does not reproduce each column's original marginal; the drift is measured and recorded in the artifact rather than hidden
b.status_of_evidence: SENSITIVITY CHECK, NOT an exact conditional randomization test — P(X_optional | X_core) is estimated, not known, so its correctness depends on the quality of g and it must never be reported as exact
b.role: run only on the survivors of A1

interpretation.a_passes_b_rejects: the A result probably came from breaking optional <-> core rather than from information in the feature
interpretation.a_rejects_b_passes: A was too conservative, or the conditional model is defective; investigate g before believing either

permutation_audit.index_hash: sha256 prefix of the block order, recorded per unit
permutation_audit._why_min_displacement: A permutation that leaves more than half the blocks in place would be a permutation in name while testing almost the original alignment.
permutation_audit._space: With ~200-300 blocks the space is ~n! and identity has probability 1/n!. The assertions cost nothing and are required anyway; the fallback of exact enumeration applies only if n_blocks ever becomes small enough for 50 draws to collide, which the recorded n_blocks per unit makes checkable.

max_null.mechanism: block shift of the whole optional-feature matrix within the discovery fold
max_null._why_not_label_shuffle: Permuting Y_outcome alone leaves the economic scoring reading non-permuted trade outcomes, so the null would not correspond to the statistic being tested. Shifting the optional block keeps core, labels and outcome intact, preserves feature autocorrelation and cross-correlation, and destroys only the temporal alignment between optional features and outcome.

block_length._status: rule frozen before any null result is seen; the value is measured under it
block_length.L_dependency: the first lag after which |ACF| stays inside the noise band for K consecutive lags
block_length._why_bars: The event rows are not an evenly spaced series — events cluster and their t0 gaps vary — so an autocorrelation computed over consecutive rows would measure event density as much as dependence. Dependence is measured in bar time and blocks are then mapped onto events through t0.
block_length._why_pair_count: A lag supported by a handful of event pairs has a huge sampling error, so it can fall inside the noise band for reasons that have nothing to do with dependence. Lags below the pair floor are inadmissible for the K-consecutive rule; per-lag lag_bars, n_event_pairs_at_lag, acf and inside_band are recorded so the rule can be audited rather than trusted. On this panel the floor H binds everywhere, so nothing here changed the value — it protects a NEW panel.
block_length._floor_is: H, the label horizon: two events closer than the horizon share outcome bars, so no block may be shorter than it
block_length._why_max: A fold must contain at least four blocks or the shift has too few positions to be a permutation at all
block_length.short_fold_behaviour: if max(H, L_dependency) exceeds a quarter of the fold, use the quarter and flag the rotation as block_length_clamped
block_length._measured: L_dependency in bar time: NVDA 24, MTD 14, GWW 7, AZO 9. The floor binds on all four — the label horizon outlasts the measured dependence everywhere, so L_block = 24 for the panel. This is expected rather than lucky: the label is built over 24 bars, so events closer than that share outcome bars whatever the ACF says.
block_length._sensitivity_rule: the verdict must not depend on one arbitrary block size; if L and 2L disagree for a candidate, that candidate is reported as block_sensitive and does not pass

max_null.pass_condition: b <= 4 after the full 50 permutations, where b = #{null statistic >= real statistic}; p_mc = (1+b)/51 <= 5/51
max_null.futility_bound: stop at b = 5 — even if every remaining permutation fell below the real value, p_mc >= 6/51 = 0.1176 and the candidate cannot pass
max_null.early_stop_reporting: verdict rejected_early, permutations performed, exceedances, and a LOWER BOUND on the final p-value. (1+b)/(1+n) must not be reported as a fixed-budget p-value for an early-stopped test.
max_null.scope: only rotations supporting a provisional acceptance — 42 rotations, 16 outer folds, 12 tickers
max_null._why_scoped: The null can only reject a provisional acceptance, never rescue a candidate that failed confirmation. Running it where nothing was accepted cannot change a decision.
max_null.controls: candidate-search, operating-point and rotation-aggregation multiplicity WITHIN one outer fold
max_null.does_not_control: the 16 outer folds and 12 tickers the test is run on
max_null._scope_statement: max-null controls candidate-search, operating-point and rotation-aggregation multiplicity within one outer fold; cross-fold and cross-asset evidence is controlled by the stability and confirmation contract. One max-null does not control the multiplicity of the project, and must never be described as if it did.
max_null._superseded_scope_statement: An earlier version of this clause said 'within a discovery rotation'. It was written when the unit was the rotation and became false when the unit became the outer fold, because the four-rotation aggregation is now INSIDE what the null reproduces. Recorded rather than deleted: a frozen document that quietly changes its own claims is worse than one that shows its corrections.

conditioning_on_acceptance._what: The 16 outer folds are tested BECAUSE the real procedure accepted something there. That selection has a consequence that must be stated wherever the results are.
conditioning_on_acceptance.still_valid: the per-fold p-value. The permutation distribution is built from that fold's own data, so the conditional argument is untouched by how the fold was chosen.
conditioning_on_acceptance.not_valid: reading the COUNT of passes across the 16 folds as a calibrated family-wise error statement, or comparing it to an expected count under H0. The folds were not sampled; they were selected for having accepted.
conditioning_on_acceptance._rule: report per-fold verdicts and per-fold p-values; never report 'x of 16 passed, expected y under the null'.

known_properties_of_the_operating_point.fold_spread_constraints: NOT applied in the quantile arm — nested_validation.choose_operating_point calls op_select.select_operating_point with min_oof_trades only, leaving min_active_folds=0 and max_fold_trade_share=1.0 at their defaults, so an operating point whose trades all come from one fold is admissible.
known_properties_of_the_operating_point._effect_on_the_test: None. The real run and every permutation take the identical code path, so this cannot bias the permutation distribution. It is a property of the PROCEDURE being defended, not of the test defending it — and a procedure whose operating point can rest on a single fold is worth knowing about when the methodology is transferred to a new panel.
known_properties_of_the_operating_point._status: recorded, not repaired: changing it now would alter the procedure mid-test.

rotation_level_null.label: Rung 5 diagnostic: rotation-level Null-A — NOT the procedure-level multiplicity verdict
role: cost benchmark for the procedure-level runner
measurement of how many conclusions change when the whole rule is aggregated instead of tested rotation by rotation
rotation_level_null._must_not: be used as the final max-null verdict
